title: mysql client returns NULL for empty string when --binary-as-hex=1
description:
There were some fixes for the mysql client printing "0x" for NULL fields, but it also looks to treat empty strings as NULL.

The behavior on 8.0.17 seems to be the correct one.
How to repeat:
On 8.0.24

mysql -P 13000 -u root -h 127.0.0.1 --binary-as-hex=1

mysql> select _binary"";
+----+
|    |
+----+
| NULL |
+----+
1 row in set (0.00 sec)

On 8.0.17

mysql -P 13000 -u root -h 127.0.0.1 --binary-as-hex=1

mysql> select _binary"";
+----+
|    |
+----+
| 0x |
+----+
key_bug_features: mysql client treats empty strings as NULL
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), data BINARY(10));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, data) VALUES (1, 'Test', _binary'');
SELECT id, name, data FROM test_table WHERE data IS NULL;
UPDATE test_table SET data = _binary'' WHERE id = 1;
SELECT _binary'' AS binary_data, IF(data IS NULL, 'NULL', 'NOT NULL') AS null_check FROM test_table;
